nodes.context: Mexican-American street and prison gang based in El Paso, Texas and Ciudad Juarez, Mexico. Involved in drug trafficking, human trafficking, and other crimes.
Armed wing of the Juárez Cartel.
Mexican drug cartel controlling smuggling routes in Ciudad Juarez.
Mexican drug cartel that attempted to take over drug smuggling routes in Ciudad Juarez.
Street gang and rival of Barrio Azteca, associated with Sinaloa Cartel.
Rival gang and enforcer group of the Sinaloa Cartel.
Cell of the Sinaloa Cartel considered on par with La Linea for control of Ciudad Juarez drug market as of June 2020.
Mexican-American street and prison gang based in El Paso, Texas and Ciudad Juarez, Mexico; involved in drug trafficking and violence.
Armed wing of the Juárez Cartel.
Mexican drug cartel operating in Ciudad Juarez.
Mexican drug cartel that attempted to take over smuggling routes in Ciudad Juarez.
Hybrid organization founded by a former Barrio Azteca member; involved in drug trafficking.
Alleged criminal group; a member was possibly targeted in a massacre.
Powerful cell of the Sinaloa Cartel operating in Ciudad Juarez.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2008, Barrio Azteca formed an alliance with La Línea , the armed wing of the Juárez Cartel , to fight off the forces of the Sinaloa Cartel
edges.context: Barrio Azteca allied with La Línea to fight the Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.context: Barrio Azteca fought against the Sinaloa Cartel.
Barrio Azteca is allied with the Juárez Cartel.

edges.context: Barrio Azteca is a rival of Los Mexicles.

edges.context: Artistas Asesinos is a rival gang to Barrio Azteca.

edges.evidence_quote: The 'life-blood' of the gang is the drug sale, which they buy at a low cost due to their alliance with the Juárez cartel
edges.context: Barrio Azteca has an alliance with the Juárez Cartel for drug purchases.

edges.evidence_quote: Mendez, who was the El Paso FBI office's most wanted fugitive and the last person sought for the attacks, also made La Empresa a hybrid of Barrio Azteca.
edges.context: La Empresa is a hybrid organization derived from Barrio Azteca.

edges.evidence_quote: Either La Línea and Barrio Azteca were responsible for the killings; or 'independent gunmen' paid to kill a person nicknamed El Ratón , an alleged member of Artistas Asesinos .
edges.context: Barrio Azteca may have been involved in a massacre targeting a member of Artistas Asesinos.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Salazar, a powerful cell of the Sinaloa Cartel, had by this point also managed to build a significant presence in Ciudad Juárez
edges.context: Los Salazar is a cell of the Sinaloa Cartel.
